stem: In a figure, lines p and q are parallel and are cut by a transversal. One same-side interior angle (between the two parallel lines, on one side of the transversal) measures 136°, and the other same-side interior angle measures x°. What is the value of x + 136?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. When two parallel lines are cut by a transversal, same-side interior angles are supplementary, so their measures sum to 180°. Therefore x + 136 = 180.

Choice A is incorrect because 44 is the value of x itself (180 - 136), not x + 136. Choice C is incorrect because 136 treats the two same-side interior angles as equal rather than supplementary. Choice D is incorrect because 272 = 2(136) treats both angles as 136°.
